headline: U.S. strikes Iran, oil surges
detail: A confirmed retaliatory U.S. strike on Iran sent oil prices higher in after-hours trading. Rising oil raises inflation expectations and tightens financial conditions, which historically pressures high-multiple tech and growth names — exactly your book. Watch for a risk-off Monday open across AVGO, PLTR, and QQQ.

headline: Tech's worst week in a year, AI momentum stalls
detail: Broad tech sold off hard this week as AI-driven momentum reversed. Your heaviest losers — AVGO and PLTR — are both directly tied to AI spending narratives. This isn't a one-day blip; the headline signals a possible sentiment shift that could keep pressure on your SELL-flagged names.

headline: Micron sinks 6%, chips under pressure
detail: Micron's sharp drop is a real-time warning for the chip sector. AVGO is your largest position and already down 16%; a sector-wide chip selloff makes the risk of further drawdown concrete, not hypothetical. SMH held better but watch it closely — it's your only chip name in the green.

headline: SpaceX bonds flash warning as AI frenzy cools
detail: Investors are pumping the brakes on SpaceX debt even as the IPO hype is fresh. You hold both SPCX (new IPO, limited data) and SPAL (2x leveraged SpaceX ETF that decays daily) — if SpaceX sentiment sours early, these positions can move faster and further than the headline numbers suggest, especially on margin.
